evals.name: backup-tool-from-notes
evals.prompt: Doc this for me. Here's my brain dump on our backup-tool CLI — turn it into proper docs under a new folder tools/backup-tool/. Notes are in agents/evals/doc-this/fixtures/backup-tool-notes.md.
evals.expected_output: A standardized folder tools/backup-tool/ with: README.md (purpose + manifest linking the deeper docs); AGENTS.md capturing the two hard rules (always --dry-run before a real restore; never delete a full snapshot); docs/*.guide.md explaining incremental snapshot chains; docs/*.reference.md for the env vars + command list; docs/*.runbook.md for the step-by-step restore. {topic}.{type}.md naming, kebab-case. No frontmatter on any docs/*.md. No content duplicated across files; each manifest link resolves.

evals.name: readme-only-minimal
evals.prompt: Can you document the scripts/ folder? All it has is one script, release-notes.sh, that prints the git log since the last tag formatted as markdown bullets. Nothing fancy. Folder: agents/evals/doc-this/fixtures/scratch-scripts/.
evals.expected_output: Just a README.md in the folder — purpose, what release-notes.sh does, and how to run it. No docs/ subfolder, no AGENTS.md, no guide/reference/runbook invented from thin material. Demonstrates restraint: orientation-only content stays in the README rather than being over-split into typed docs.

evals.name: fold-into-existing
evals.prompt: Update the docs in agents/evals/doc-this/fixtures/existing-widget/ — we just added a rule that widgets wider or taller than 4000px are rejected with an error. Fold that in.
evals.expected_output: The new 4000px limit added to the existing docs/widget-config.reference.md (the right home for a spec fact), not a new parallel file and not duplicated into the README. README manifest left intact. No frontmatter introduced. Output stays conformant and idempotent — structure unchanged apart from the added fact.

evals.name: no-input-asks-for-material
evals.expected_output: No source material was provided, so the skill asks the user what to document — it requests the raw material (notes, transcript, description, existing docs) first. It writes no files and does not scan or read unrelated repo files to invent a subject. Baseline (no skill) is expected to start scanning the repo, so this case discriminates.

evals.name: suggests-home-folder-awaits-approval
evals.prompt: Doc this for me — here's my brain dump on our backup-tool CLI. Notes are in agents/evals/doc-this/fixtures/backup-tool-notes.md.
evals.expected_output: Material is provided but no target folder is named, so the skill proposes a single home folder (e.g. tools/backup-tool/) with a clear reason for the choice and level, then waits for the user's approval before writing anything. It does not silently create a folder and dump docs. Baseline (no skill) is expected to write docs without asking for placement approval, so this case discriminates.

evals.name: reconcile-removal-from-change
evals.prompt: We dropped SVG support from the widget exporter — it's PNG-only now. Update the docs in agents/evals/doc-this/fixtures/existing-widget/ to match.
evals.expected_output: A change is stated and the folder is named and already documented, so the skill reconciles in place — it does NOT ask for raw material first and does NOT run a home-folder approval round-trip (both gates apply to authoring from scratch, not to syncing a named, documented folder). The stale SVG mention is removed: docs/widget-config.reference.md's format note becomes PNG-only and the README summary line stops saying PNG/SVG. No new or parallel file, README manifest left intact, no frontmatter introduced. Minimal and idempotent — only the SVG mentions change. This is primarily a regression guard for the change-driven path: a capable model on the old skill can also execute this once invoked (the folder is named), so the change's discriminating value is in triggering — the description now advertises change-driven use — which is tested separately, not by this execution case.

evals.name: triggers-on-brief-mention
evals.prompt: oh and while you're in there — can you document the references for the tools/backup-tool/ folder? just write it up properly.
evals.expected_output: A TRIGGERING case, not an execution-quality one. With the doc-this skill installed and NOT suppressed or instructed, the model spontaneously invokes Skill(doc-this) from this terse, oblique aside — the kind of brief mid-task mention that previously slipped past — and follows the skill's flow instead of hand-writing ad-hoc docs. A skill-suppressed baseline does not invoke it and free-forms a doc instead, so this case discriminates on triggering. NOTE: the real discriminating condition — a half-full context window where the brief mention competes for attention — is a run-time setup, not encoded in this prompt string; grade by observing whether Skill(doc-this) fires (spontaneous firing is observable in this repo, per the iteration-2 baseline-contamination note). 'references' here means the folder's source material/citations and must route to doc-this, NOT to redirect-bookmark (which is for a single external URL / Drive link).
